expectations.text: behavioral_check: Identifies that set_group_by_electrode_group groups by electrode_group_name (one sort group per electrode_group)
expectations.evidence: States: 'walks np.unique(electrodes["electrode_group_name"]) and inserts one sort group per electrode group'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Explains the clusterless failure: multi-shank electrode_groups produce duplicate (x, y) contact positions; SpikeInterface rejects at SpikeSortingRecording.populate()
expectations.evidence: Section 2 quotes exact mechanism: 'Sort groups spanning multiple shanks produce duplicate (x, y) contact positions, which SpikeInterface rejects at SpikeSortingRecording.populate with ValueError: contact positions are not unique.'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends set_group_by_shank for clusterless / waveform-based pipelines
expectations.evidence: Final recommendation: `SortGroup().set_group_by_shank(nwb_file_name=nwb_file)` for v1 clusterless/sorted-spikes workflow.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Notes that set_group_by_electrode_group is only available in the v0 API (spyglass.spikesorting.v0), not in v1
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'set_group_by_electrode_group is a v0-only method. It does not exist on the v1 SortGroup class' with src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:50-95 citation.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not claim set_group_by_electrode_group produces one electrode per sort group (it groups by electrode_group_name, which can include multiple electrodes per group — that is precisely the source of the multi-shank conflict)
expectations.evidence: Correctly states one electrode group becomes one sort group containing all that group's channels (potentially multiple shanks).
